title: Ryan Coogler Takes Home Oscar For Best Original Screenplay
text: At the 98th Academy Awards on March 15, 2026, filmmaker Ryan Coogler won the Academy Award for Best Original Screenplay for his film "Sinners". This marked his first Oscar win and Warner Bros.' first victory of the evening. Acceptance Speech Highlights: Coogler received a standing ovation and opened his remarks with characteristic warmth. "Please sit down, I'm very nervous and they are going to play me off," he said, adding "I'm from Oakland, California and we can talk a lot." The director thanked studio leadership, his cast, and crew, asking them to stand and receive recognition. He extended special gratitude to his family, apologizing for time spent away from them during production. Most touchingly, Coogler addressed his children directly, stating: "I love you more than anything." He reflected on the importance of memories, hoping the achievement would be a cherished moment they'd remember. Path to Victory: Coogler's win came after he had previously secured the Writers Guild Award for the screenplay, making him the frontrunner heading into Oscar night. Robert Downey Jr. and Chris Evans presented the award in a mini-Avengers reunion moment.
